query: Test whether sample [68,74,71,75,69,73,72,70] supports the claim that adult resting heart rate is 72 bpm at alpha=0.05. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call perform_t_test tool to compare the sample mean against the claimed population mean.

Identify anomalous measurements in [10.1,10.3,10.0,9.9,10.2,14.8,10.1] before release. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call detect_outliers tool to flag unusually extreme observations.

Check whether exam scores [72,75,74,78,73,76,77,74,75,73] satisfy the normality assumption. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call perform_normality_test tool to determine whether a parametric analysis is appropriate.

Provide a 99% confidence interval for mean blood pressure from [120,118,125,122,119,123,121]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_confidence_interval tool to estimate the range for the true mean at 99% confidence.

Convert quiz scores [55, 70, 85, 90, 60] into z-scores. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_z_scores tool to standardize scores for comparison across sections.

Build a linear regression model from ad spend x=[1,2,3,4,5] and sales y=[120,150,180,200,230]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call perform_linear_regression tool to estimate the relationship for forecasting sales from spend.

Smooth [22,25,23,28,30,27,31,29,33,35] with a 4-period moving average. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_moving_average tool to reduce short-term fluctuations in the sensor readings.

Quantify co-movement between study hours [2,3,5,6,7] and exam scores [55,65,80,88,92]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_covariance tool to measure how the two variables vary together.

Compute skewness for [48000,51000,49000,52000,50000,53000,480000,520000]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_skewness tool to quantify how strongly the distribution leans to one side.

Summarize [1200,1350,980,1100,1420,1300,900,1250,1180,1400,1050,1320,970,1230]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call generate_descriptive_statistics tool to produce a compact overview of the dataset.

Compute kurtosis for returns [0.02,-0.01,0.03,0.015,-0.005,0.025,0.01,-0.02,0.018,0.012]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_kurtosis tool to assess whether the distribution has heavier or lighter tails than normal.

Return Q1, median, and Q3 for [24,18,36,22,19,42,21,28,17,35,20,30]. Break down the problem into simpler, smaller subproblems if necessary. Step 1: call calculate_quartiles tool to describe the spread of the ordered delivery times.
